question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the health() endpoint repeatedly to observe whether the monitor is actively publishing transactions, and use that timing information to infer when scheduled transactions lacking all required signatories are being submitted to the network, causing unauthorized transaction execution without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network-adjacent position spoof the HTTP response from the /api/v1/network/stake endpoint to return a 2xx status code, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return Status.UP and thereby allowing the monitor to proceed with publishing transactions that have not been approved by all required signers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted HTTP 200 response into the network stake check by exploiting the absence of response body validation in restNetworkStakeHealth(), causing the health gate to open and authorize transaction publishing without verifying that all transaction signatories have approved?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the restNetworkStakeHealth() timeout of Duration.ofSeconds(5) to be exploited by delaying the network stake response just under 5 seconds, keeping the health check in a perpetual UNKNOWN state that prevents DOWN status from being set, thereby allowing transactions to continue being published without proper signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorResume() fallback in restNetworkStakeHealth() by causing a non-ConnectException, non-TimeoutException error, which defaults to Status.UNKNOWN rather than Status.DOWN, allowing the health() method to proceed with publishing() and subscribing() checks and potentially authorize transactions without all required signatories?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() has no authentication or rate limiting, polling it at high frequency to cause reactive stream back-pressure issues that corrupt the health state machine, leading to a false UP status that authorizes transaction publishing without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static AtomicInteger CLUSTER_UP field shared across all class instances by triggering concurrent health() calls that race in recordHealthMetric(), causing CLUSTER_UP to be set to 1 (UP) even when the actual health is DOWN, thereby signaling to dependent systems that transactions can be authorized without proper signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition on the static CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger in recordHealthMetric() by flooding the health endpoint with concurrent requests, causing the metric to reflect UP status while actual transaction authorization checks are incomplete, leading to unauthorized transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the scenario rate reported by transactionGenerator.scenarios() through an exposed configuration or REST endpoint, causing publishing() to see a sum > 0 and return an empty Mono, which then triggers subscribing() to return UP, ultimately authorizing transaction publishing without verifying signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publishing() method's filter(sum -> sum <= 0) logic by injecting a scenario with a positive rate into the transactionGenerator, bypassing the zero-rate gate and causing the health check to skip the DOWN/UNKNOWN path, thereby allowing transactions to be published without all required signatories having signed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subscribing() method's filter(sum -> sum > 0) check by causing a subscription scenario to report a non-zero rate through a spoofed subscription response, causing subscribing() to return Status.UP and authorize transaction publishing even when the underlying transactions lack required owner signatures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() flux to emit a scenario with an artificially inflated rate by replaying or injecting subscription responses, making subscribing() return UP and triggering the monitor to publish transactions that have not been approved by all required signers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getHealthForZeroRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getHealthForZeroRate() method's dependency on releaseHealthProperties.isFailWhenInactive() by observing that when failWhenInactive=false the system returns UNKNOWN instead of DOWN, and use this to keep the monitor in a state where it continues publishing transactions with insufficient signatories without triggering a hard failure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap chain in health() where publishing().switchIfEmpty(subscribing()) is only evaluated when restNetworkStakeHealth() returns UP, by forcing the network stake endpoint to always return 2xx, ensuring the publishing/subscribing path is always taken and transactions are authorized regardless of whether signers have approved?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a DNS rebinding attack against the REST API client's base URL used in restNetworkStakeHealth(), causing getNetworkStakeStatusCode() to resolve to an attacker-controlled server that always returns HTTP 200, making the health check always pass and authorizing transaction publishing without proper signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restNetworkStakeHealth() only checks statusCode.is2xxSuccessful() and statusCode.is5xxServerError() without validating the response body, allowing a 200 response with a malformed or empty body to pass the health gate and authorize transactions that lack required owner signatures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an HTTP 3xx redirect from the network stake endpoint to an attacker-controlled server that returns 200, bypassing the health check and causing the monitor to authorize and publish transactions without verifying that all required signatories have approved?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any cryptographic verification in the health() pipeline to inject a fake health status by manipulating the reactive stream, causing the monitor to believe the cluster is UP and proceed to publish scheduled transactions without all required signers having provided approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user access the Micrometer metrics endpoint exposing the hiero.mirror.monitor.health gauge registered in registerGauge(), read the CLUSTER_UP value to determine when the monitor is actively publishing, and time an attack to submit a ScheduleSign transaction with insufficient signatories during the UP window, causing unauthorized transaction execution?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly accessible Micrometer gauge hiero.mirror.monitor.health with tag type=cluster to monitor the CLUSTER_UP state transitions, inferring when the monitor's operator key is actively signing transactions, and use that window to front-run or replay transactions without the operator's explicit per-transaction approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator health endpoint that invokes health() without requiring authentication, repeatedly querying it to enumerate the internal state of transaction publishing scenarios and subscription rates, then use that information to identify windows where transactions are being submitted without all required signatories?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the reduce(0.0, (c, n) -> c + n) aggregation in publishing() to overflow or produce unexpected results by injecting scenarios with extreme rate values (e.g., Double.MAX_VALUE or NaN), causing the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) check to behave unexpectedly and bypass the zero-rate gate, authorizing transaction publishing without proper signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario with a NaN rate into transactionGenerator.scenarios(), causing the reduce(0.0, (c, n) -> c + n) sum to become NaN, which fails the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) check (since NaN <= 0 is false), making publishing() return an empty Mono and triggering subscribing() to return UP, thereby authorizing transactions without verifying signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario with a NaN or Infinity rate into mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions(), causing the reduce(0.0, (cur, next) -> cur + next) sum to be NaN or Infinity, which passes the filter(sum -> sum > 0) check, making subscribing() return Status.UP and authorizing transaction publishing without all required owner signatures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Mono chain in health() by causing the doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) side effect to execute with a null Health object (since recordHealthMetric handles null by defaulting to Status.UP), setting CLUSTER_UP=1 and signaling to dependent systems that transactions are authorized even when the actual health check has not completed?
